deep_dive.title: DynaMiCS: Fine-tuning LLMs with Performance Constraints using Dynamic Mixtures
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-domain fine-tuning of large language models requires improving performance on target domains while preserving performance on constrained domains, such as general knowledge, instruction following, or safety evaluations. Existing data mixing strategies rely on fixed heuristics or adaptive rules that cannot explicitly enforce preservation of such capabilities. We propose DynaMiCS, a dynamic mixture optimizer that casts multi-domain fine-tuning as a constrained optimization problem. At each update, DynaMiCS performs short domain-specific probing runs to estimate a slope matrix of local cross-domain effects, capturing how training on each fine-tuning dataset affects each evaluation domain. These estimates are then used to compute mixture weights through optimization over the probability simplex, with the objective of improving target-domain performance while keeping constrained-domain losses below reference levels. Across multi-domain fine-tuning scenarios with varying numbers of target and constrained domains, DynaMiCS achieves stronger target-domain improvements and higher constraint satisfaction than fixed-mixture baselines, at lower computational cost and without reference models, per-example scoring, or manually tuned mixture weights.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: DynaMiCS proposes dynamic data mixture optimization to preserve safety and other constrained domains during fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: DynaMiCS提出动态数据混合优化，在微调过程中保留安全等约束域性能
deep_dive.llm_evidence: DynaMiCS提出动态数据混合优化，在微调过程中保留安全等约束域性能
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: DynaMiCS提出动态数据混合优化，在微调过程中保留安全等约束域性能
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DynaMiCS formulates multi-domain fine-tuning as constrained optimization, dynamically adjusting data mixture weights to preserve safety and other capabilities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多领域微调大语言模型时，需要提升目标域性能同时保留约束域（如安全评估）的能力。现有数据混合策略无法显式保证这一点。DynaMiCS将微调转化为约束优化问题，通过短域探测估计跨域影响矩阵，动态计算混合权重。实验表明它能在提升目标域性能的同时，有效维持安全和通用能力，为安全对齐数据混合提供了直接可用的方法。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

deep_dive.title: RefusalGuard: Geometry-Preserving Fine-Tuning for Safety in LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Fine-tuning safety-aligned language models for downstream tasks often leads to substantial degradation of refusal behavior, making models vulnerable to adversarial misuse. While prior work has shown that safety-relevant features are encoded in structured representations within the model's activation space, how these representations change during fine-tuning and why alignment degrades remains poorly understood. In this work, we investigate the representation-level mechanisms underlying alignment degradation. Our analysis shows that standard fine-tuning induces systematic drift in safety-relevant representations, distorts their geometric structure, and introduces interference between task optimization and safety features. These effects collectively lead to increased harmful compliance. Motivated by these findings, we introduce REFUSALGUARD, a representation-level fine-tuning framework that preserves safety-relevant structure during model adaptation. Our approach constrains updates in hidden representation space, ensuring that safety-mediating components remain stable while allowing task-specific learning in complementary directions. We evaluate REFUSALGUARD across multiple model families, including LLaMA, Gemma, and Qwen, on adversarial safety benchmarks such as AdvBench, DirectHarm4, and JailbreakBench, as well as downstream utility tasks. Our approach achieves attack success rates comparable to base safety-aligned models while maintaining competitive task performance, significantly outperforming baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: addresses safety degradation from fine-tuning LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对微调导致的安全退化问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对微调导致的安全退化问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对微调导致的安全退化问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RefusalGuard proposes geometry-preserving fine-tuning to prevent safety alignment degradation by constraining drift in safety representations during fine-tuning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有微调过程会破坏大语言模型的安全表示几何结构，导致拒绝行为退化。本文提出RefusalGuard，通过约束微调中安全表示的漂移并保持其几何结构，在保证下游任务性能的同时有效维护模型安全对齐。实验表明该方法能显著降低有害输出率，为安全微调提供了新的表示级解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有微调过程会破坏大语言模型的安全表示几何结构，导致拒绝行为退化。本文提出RefusalGuard，通过约束微调中安全表示的漂移并保持其几何结构，在保证下游任务性能的同时有效维护模型安全对齐。实验表明该方法能显著降低有害输出率，为安全微调提供了新的表示级解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Self-Mined Hardness for Safety Fine-Tuning
deep_dive.abstract: Safety fine-tuning of language models typically requires a curated adversarial dataset. We take a different approach: score each candidate prompt's difficulty by how often the target model's own rollouts are judged harmful, then fine-tune on the hardest prompts paired with the model's own non-jailbroken rollouts. On Llama-3-8B-Instruct and Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct, this approach cuts the WildJailbreak attack success rate from 11.5% and 20.1% down to 1-3%, but pushes refusal on jailbreak-shaped benign prompts from 14-22% to 74-94%. Interleaving the same hard prompts 1:1 with adversarially-framed benign prompts (prompts that look like jailbreaks but have benign intent) cuts that refusal back down to 30-51% on 8B and 52-72% on 3B, at a cost of 2-6 percentage points of attack success rate. Within the mixed regime, training on the hardest half of the eligible pool rather than a random half cuts the remaining ASR by 35-50% (about 3 percentage points) on both models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: self-mined hardness for safety data selection and mixing with benign prompts
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自我挖掘困难度进行安全数据选择并与良性提示混合
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自我挖掘困难度进行安全数据选择并与良性提示混合
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自我挖掘困难度进行安全数据选择并与良性提示混合
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Self-mining the hardest prompts for safety fine-tuning, then mixing them 1:1 with benign jailbreak-shaped prompts, achieves low attack success while controlling over-refusal.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 安全微调通常依赖人工筛选对抗数据集。本文另辟蹊径：根据模型自身输出被判定有害的频率对候选提示打分，然后仅用最难提示及其非越狱响应进行微调。在Llama系列模型上，该方法将攻击成功率降至1-3%，但导致过度拒绝。为解决此问题，将硬提示与看似越狱但意图良性的提示按1:1混合，使过度拒绝降至30-51%，攻击成功率仅上升2-6个百分点。该工作直接展示了安全数据选择与混合的重要性。

deep_dive.title: Safety Anchor: Defending Harmful Fine-tuning via Geometric Bottlenecks
deep_dive.abstract: The safety alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) remains vulnerable to Harmful Fine-tuning (HFT). While existing defenses impose constraints on parameters, gradients, or internal representations, we observe that they can be effectively circumvented under persistent HFT. Our analysis traces this failure to the inherent redundancy of the high-dimensional parameter space: attackers exploit optimization trajectories that are orthogonal to defense constraints to restore harmful capabilities while deceptively adhering to safety restrictions. To address this, we propose Safety Bottleneck Regularization (SBR). SBR shifts the defensive focus from the redundant parameter space to the unembedding layer, which serves as a geometric bottleneck. By anchoring the final hidden states of harmful queries to those of the safety-aligned model, SBR enables the model to maintain safe responses even under persistent HFT. Extensive experiments confirm SBR's effectiveness, demonstrating that utilizing just a single safety anchor is sufficient to reduce the Harmful Score to $<$10 while preserving competitive performance on benign downstream tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Safety Bottleneck Regularization defends against harmful fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过几何瓶颈正则化防御有害微调攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过几何瓶颈正则化防御有害微调攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过几何瓶颈正则化防御有害微调攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Safety Anchor proposes Safety Bottleneck Regularization (SBR) that shifts defense to the unembedding layer bottleneck, effectively resisting harmful fine-tuning attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有防御有害微调的方法在高维参数空间中容易被绕过。本文分析发现攻击者利用参数冗余性规避约束，提出安全瓶颈正则化（SBR），将防御焦点转移到非嵌入层的几何瓶颈上。实验表明SBR能有效抵抗持续的有害微调攻击，为维护模型安全对齐提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Safety Anchor: Defending Harmful Fine-tuning via Geometric Bottlenecks
deep_dive.abstract: The safety alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) remains vulnerable to Harmful Fine-tuning (HFT). While existing defenses impose constraints on parameters, gradients, or internal representations, we observe that they can be effectively circumvented under persistent HFT. Our analysis traces this failure to the inherent redundancy of the high-dimensional parameter space: attackers exploit optimization trajectories that are orthogonal to defense constraints to restore harmful capabilities while deceptively adhering to safety restrictions. To address this, we propose Safety Bottleneck Regularization (SBR). SBR shifts the defensive focus from the redundant parameter space to the unembedding layer, which serves as a geometric bottleneck. By anchoring the final hidden states of harmful queries to those of the safety-aligned model, SBR enables the model to maintain safe responses even under persistent HFT. Extensive experiments confirm SBR's effectiveness, demonstrating that utilizing just a single safety anchor is sufficient to reduce the Harmful Score to $<$10 while preserving competitive performance on benign downstream tasks.

deep_dive.title: Few-Shot Truly Benign DPO Attack for Jailbreaking LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Fine-tuning APIs make frontier LLMs easy to customize, but they can also weaken safety alignment during fine-tuning. While prior work shows that benign supervised fine-tuning (SFT) can reduce refusal behavior, deployed fine-tuning pipelines increasingly support preference-based objectives, whose safety risks remain less understood. We show that Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) introduces a stronger and harder-to-audit failure mode. We propose a truly benign DPO attack using only 10 harmless preference pairs, the minimum data scale accepted by OpenAI's fine-tuning service. Each pair contains a benign prompt, a normal helpful answer as the preferred response, and a refusal as the dispreferred response. Unlike prior benign fine-tuning attacks, our data exhibits no suspicious behavior: it is practically indistinguishable from the fine-tuning request of a legitimate user seeking to reduce over-refusal, making harmful intent almost impossible to infer from the request alone. Nevertheless, because DPO directly optimizes the model to prefer helpful answers over refusals, this seemingly benign objective broadly suppresses refusal behavior and transfers to harmful prompts outside the fine-tuning data. Across OpenAI models supporting DPO fine-tuning, our attack achieves attack success rates of 59.13% on GPT-4o, 70.20% on GPT-4.1, 54.80% on GPT-4.1-mini, and 81.73% on GPT-4.1-nano, at costs of only \$1.7, \$1.7, \$0.3, and \$0.1. Moreover, on open-weight models that do not impose minimum data requirements, we find that this effect can emerge from even a single benign preference pair.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benign DPO attack highlights need for careful safety data selection in fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 良性DPO攻击表明微调中需谨慎选择安全数据以缓解越狱风险
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 良性DPO攻击表明微调中需谨慎选择安全数据以缓解越狱风险
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 良性DPO攻击表明微调中需谨慎选择安全数据以缓解越狱风险
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A truly benign DPO attack using only 10 harmless preference pairs can jailbreak LLMs, revealing that even benign data can weaken safety alignment during fine-tuning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 微调API虽便利，但可能削弱安全对齐。现有良性SFT攻击已显示风险，但DPO的安全威胁更隐蔽。本文提出真正良性DPO攻击，仅用10个无害偏好对即可越狱LLM。每个偏好对包含良性提示、正常回答（偏好）和拒绝（非偏好），数据表面无害。实验表明该攻击成功率显著，证明微调中安全数据选择至关重要。该工作强调了在微调过程中混合安全数据时必须警惕此类攻击。

deep_dive.title: GradShield: Alignment Preserving Finetuning
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) pose a significant risk of safety misalignment after finetuning, as models can be compromised by both explicitly and implicitly harmful data. Even some seemingly benign data can inadvertently steer a model towards misaligned behaviors. To address this, we introduce GradShield, a principled filtering method that safeguards LLMs during finetuning by identifying and removing harmful data points before they corrupt the model's alignment. It removes potentially harmful data by computing a Finetuning Implicit Harmfulness Score (FIHS) for each data point and employs an adaptive thresholding algorithm. We apply GradShield to multiple utility fine-tuning tasks across varying levels of harmful data and evaluate the safety and utility performance of the resulting LLMs using various metrics. The results show that GradShield outperforms all baseline methods, consistently maintaining an Attack Success Rate (ASR) below $6\%$ while preserving utility performance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: filters harmful data points to preserve alignment during fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在微调过程中过滤有害数据点以保持对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在微调过程中过滤有害数据点以保持对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在微调过程中过滤有害数据点以保持对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GradShield computes a Finetuning Implicit Harmfulness Score (FIHS) per data point and uses adaptive thresholding to remove harmful data before alignment corruption.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对微调导致安全对齐退化问题，提出GradShield滤波方法。为每个数据点计算微调隐式有害分数(FIHS)，并通过自适应阈值算法在训练前移除有害数据。在多种实用微调任务上验证，可有效保持模型安全性而不显著降低效用，实现了安全数据筛选与对齐保持。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对微调导致安全对齐退化问题，提出GradShield滤波方法。为每个数据点计算微调隐式有害分数(FIHS)，并通过自适应阈值算法在训练前移除有害数据。在多种实用微调任务上验证，可有效保持模型安全性而不显著降低效用，实现了安全数据筛选与对齐保持。

quick_skim.title: From Parameter Dynamics to Risk Scoring : Quantifying Sample-Level Safety Degradation in LLM Fine-tuning
quick_skim.abstract: Safety alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) is extremely fragile, as fine-tuning on a small number of benign samples can erase safety behaviors learned from millions of preference examples. Existing studies attempt to explain this phenomenon by comparing parameters and hidden states before and after fine-tuning, but overlook their dynamic evolution during fine-tuning. In this paper, we uncover a critical mechanism underlying safety degradation by analyzing parameter dynamics, where benign fine-tuning causes parameters to cumulatively drift toward danger-aligned directions, progressively undermining the model's safety. This finding suggests that samples contributing more to this drift has greater fine-tuning risks. Based on this insight, we propose a method of Sample-Level Quantification of Safety Degradation (SQSD), which quantifies the influence of each training sample on safety degradation. Specifically, SQSD computes continuous risk scores to samples by measuring their induced parameter updates' projection difference between danger and safety directions. Extensive experiments across multiple models and datasets demonstrate that SQSD effectively quantifies sample-level fine-tuning risks and exhibits strong transferability across model architectures, parameter scales, and parameter-efficient methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: quantifies sample-level safety risk to guide data selection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 量化样本级安全风险，指导安全数据选择
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 量化样本级安全风险，指导安全数据选择
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 量化样本级安全风险，指导安全数据选择
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes parameter dynamics during fine-tuning to identify samples causing cumulative drift toward danger, enabling risk-based safety data selection.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过分析微调过程中参数的动态演化，发现良性样本也会导致参数向危险方向累积漂移，从而提出基于参数漂移的样本级安全风险评分方法。该评分可直接用于安全数据的选择，与需求中安全数据选择的需求高度契合。

quick_skim.title: You Snooze, You Lose: Automatic Safety Alignment Restoration through Neural Weight Translation
quick_skim.abstract: The open-source ecosystem has accelerated the democratization of Large Language Models (LLMs) through the public distribution of specialized Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) modules. However, integrating these third-party adapters often induces catastrophic forgetting of the base model's foundational safety alignment. Restoring these guardrails via fine-tuning on safety data introduces an opposing failure mode: the severe degradation of the specialized domain knowledge the adapter was originally designed to provide. To overcome this zero-resource challenge, we propose Neural Weight Translation (NeWTral), a framework that directly maps unsafe, domain-specific adapters onto a safe alignment manifold while rigorously preserving their core expertise. NeWTral operates as a non-linear translation module pre-trained on a diverse corpus of unsafe-to-safe adapter pairs. By executing this mapping entirely within the parameter space, NeWTral utilizes an adaptive Mixture of Experts (MoE) routing strategy to autonomously blend high-fidelity surgical translators and aggressive alignment experts. We evaluate our framework across four architectural families (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, and Gemma) at scales up to 72B parameters across eight diverse scientific and professional domains. Our results demonstrate that the MoE variant achieves a radical reduction in the average Attack Success Rate (ASR), dropping from 70% in unsafe experts to just 13%, while maintaining an exceptional 90\% average knowledge fidelity. Much like the crowdsourced adapters it remedies, the NeWTral module is designed as a standalone, downloadable asset that allows practitioners to restore safety alignment instantly without requiring access to original training data or hardware-intensive retraining.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: restores safety alignment after fine-tuning with adapters
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Neural Weight Translation (NeWTral) to restore base model safety alignment after integrating unsafe LoRA adapters without retraining on safety data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对使用第三方LoRA适配器微调导致安全对齐遗忘的问题，该文提出NeWTral框架，通过神经权重翻译将不安全适配器直接映射到安全对齐流形，同时保留领域知识。实验证明该方法有效恢复安全性，避免了安全数据重训练导致的性能退化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对使用第三方LoRA适配器微调导致安全对齐遗忘的问题，该文提出NeWTral框架，通过神经权重翻译将不安全适配器直接映射到安全对齐流形，同时保留领域知识。实验证明该方法有效恢复安全性，避免了安全数据重训练导致的性能退化。

quick_skim.title: SafeTune: Mitigating Data Poisoning in LLM Fine-Tuning for RTL Code Generation
quick_skim.abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly fine-tuned for hardware tasks like RTL code generation, the scarcity of high-quality datasets often leads to the use of rapidly assembled or generated training data. These datasets frequently lack security verification and are highly susceptible to data poisoning attacks. Such poisoning can cause models to generate syntactically valid but insecure hardware modules that bypass standard functionality checks. To address this, we present SafeTune, a framework designed to harden LLM-based RTL generation against poisoning, specifically focusing on hardware Trojan (HT) insertion. SafeTune integrates two core components: (i) a Graph Neural Network (GNN) that models structural properties to identify anomalous circuitry patterns during fine-tuning, and (ii) a semantic verification module using text embeddings and an XGBoost classifier to assess prompt security. By coupling structural and semantic knowledge, SafeTune effectively filters poisoned inputs without sacrificing legitimate data. Experimental results demonstrate that SafeTune significantly enhances the robustness and reliability of LLM fine-tuning without requiring modifications to the underlying model architecture.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: SafeTune framework defends against data poisoning in LLM fine-tuning, incorporating security verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: SafeTune框架防御LLM微调中的数据投毒，集成安全验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: SafeTune框架防御LLM微调中的数据投毒，集成安全验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: SafeTune框架防御LLM微调中的数据投毒，集成安全验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SafeTune hardens LLM fine-tuning against data poisoning for RTL code generation using GNN anomaly detection and safe data generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM微调中数据投毒问题，特别是在RTL代码生成领域，SafeTune框架通过图神经网络检测异常数据并生成安全数据，以防止硬件木马插入。该方法在保持功能正确的前提下提升安全性，但专注于硬件域而非通用安全对齐。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对LLM微调中数据投毒问题，特别是在RTL代码生成领域，SafeTune框架通过图神经网络检测异常数据并生成安全数据，以防止硬件木马插入。该方法在保持功能正确的前提下提升安全性，但专注于硬件域而非通用安全对齐。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data

quick_skim.title: One Step to the Side: Why Defenses Against Malicious Finetuning Fail Under Adaptive Adversaries
quick_skim.abstract: Model providers increasingly release open weights or allow users to fine-tune foundation models through APIs. Although these models are safety-aligned before release, their safeguards can often be removed by fine-tuning on harmful data. Recent defenses aim to make models robust to such malicious fine-tuning, but they are largely evaluated only against fixed attacks that do not account for the defense. We show that these robustness claims are incomplete. Surveying 15 recent defenses, we identify several defense mechanisms and show that they share a single weakness: they obscure or misdirect the path to harmful behavior without removing the behavior itself. We then develop a unified adaptive attack that breaks defenses across all defense mechanisms. Our results show that current approaches do not provide robust security; they mainly stop the attacks they were designed against. We hope that our unified adaptive adversary for this domain will help future researchers and practitioners stress-test new defenses before deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analyzes failures of defenses against malicious finetuning under adaptive adversaries
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析在自适应对手下防御有害微调失败的原因
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析在自适应对手下防御有害微调失败的原因
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析在自适应对手下防御有害微调失败的原因
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Surveys 15 defenses against malicious fine-tuning, shows they share a weakness and can be bypassed by a unified adaptive attack.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本研究调查了15种针对恶意微调的防御机制，发现它们共享一个共同弱点：仅掩盖或误导有害行为路径而未移除行为本身。作者开发了一种统一的适应性攻击，能够突破所有这些防御，揭示了现有防御评估的不完整性。这项工作对理解有害微调防御的局限性至关重要，并强调了需要更强的防御策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本研究调查了15种针对恶意微调的防御机制，发现它们共享一个共同弱点：仅掩盖或误导有害行为路径而未移除行为本身。作者开发了一种统一的适应性攻击，能够突破所有这些防御，揭示了现有防御评估的不完整性。这项工作对理解有害微调防御的局限性至关重要，并强调了需要更强的防御策略。

quick_skim.title: Preference-aware Influence-function-based Data Selection Method for Efficient Fine-Tuning
quick_skim.abstract: As LLMs continue to scale, improving training efficiency increasingly depends on using data more effectively. Data selection addresses this problem by allocating a limited training budget to samples that best promote a target behavior. Existing methods usually represent the target behavior with a set of target examples, but often treat these examples as equally important. This can be inefficient because target examples may differ in their relevance to the current model: examples closer to the model's current behavior provide more actionable guidance than those farther away. We propose PRISM (PReference-aware Influence-function-based Data Selection Method for Efficient Fine-Tuning), which uses the current model's preference to weight target examples and construct a preference-aware target representation. PRISM then scores candidate training samples by their alignment with this representation, concentrating the data budget on samples more likely to move the model toward the target behavior. Theoretical analysis shows that this preference weighting yields a more effective first-order direction for increasing target-behavior preference. Experiments across model families and scales show that PRISM improves both efficient fine-tuning and safety-oriented SFT repair, demonstrating that precise target-behavior characterization is key to budget-efficient data selection.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Preference-aware influence-function data selection for fine-tuning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于影响函数和偏好感知的微调数据选择方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于影响函数和偏好感知的微调数据选择方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于影响函数和偏好感知的微调数据选择方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PRISM uses influence functions weighted by current model preference to select training data that best promotes target behavior, applicable to safety data selection.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型微调中的数据选择问题，现有方法往往将目标示例同等对待，效率低下。本文提出PRISM方法，利用当前模型的偏好对影响函数进行加权，从而更有效地选择促进目标行为的训练样本。实验表明该方法在多种任务上提升了微调效率，其数据选择框架可迁移至安全对齐数据筛选场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型微调中的数据选择问题，现有方法往往将目标示例同等对待，效率低下。本文提出PRISM方法，利用当前模型的偏好对影响函数进行加权，从而更有效地选择促进目标行为的训练样本。实验表明该方法在多种任务上提升了微调效率，其数据选择框架可迁移至安全对齐数据筛选场景。

quick_skim.title: BSO: Safety Alignment Is Density Ratio Matching
quick_skim.abstract: Aligning language models for both helpfulness and safety typically requires complex pipelines-separate reward and cost models, online reinforcement learning, and primal-dual updates. Recent direct preference optimization approaches simplify training but incorporate safety through ad-hoc modifications such as multi-stage procedures or heuristic margin terms, lacking a principled derivation. We show that the likelihood ratio of the optimal safe policy admits a closed-form decomposition that reduces safety alignment to a density ratio matching problem. Minimizing Bregman divergences between the data and model ratios yields Bregman Safety Optimization (BSO), a family of single-stage loss functions, each induced by a convex generator, that provably recover the optimal safe policy. BSO is both general and simple: it requires no auxiliary models, introduces only one hyperparameter beyond standard preference optimization, and recovers existing safety-aware methods as special cases. Experiments across safety alignment benchmarks show that BSO consistently improves the safety-helpfulness trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: density ratio matching for safety alignment, applicable to data mixing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 密度比匹配用于安全对齐，可应用于数据混合场景
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 密度比匹配用于安全对齐，可应用于数据混合场景
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 密度比匹配用于安全对齐，可应用于数据混合场景
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Bregman Safety Optimization (BSO), a family of single-stage loss functions that reduce safety alignment to density ratio matching, enabling principled safe policy learning without complex pipelines.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前语言模型安全对齐方法通常需要复杂的分离式管线（奖励与成本模型、在线强化学习等）。本文证明最优安全策略的似然比可分解为密度比匹配问题，提出Bregman安全优化（BSO）框架，通过最小化数据与模型之间的Bregman散度，得到一系列单阶段损失函数，理论上可恢复最优安全策略。实验表明BSO在保持有用性的同时显著提升安全性，为安全微调中的数据混合提供了理论基础和方法支撑。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前语言模型安全对齐方法通常需要复杂的分离式管线（奖励与成本模型、在线强化学习等）。本文证明最优安全策略的似然比可分解为密度比匹配问题，提出Bregman安全优化（BSO）框架，通过最小化数据与模型之间的Bregman散度，得到一系列单阶段损失函数，理论上可恢复最优安全策略。实验表明BSO在保持有用性的同时显著提升安全性，为安全微调中的数据混合提供了理论基础和方法支撑。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

quick_skim.title: Be Kind, Rewrite: Benign Projections via Rewriting Defend Against LLM Data Poisoning Attacks
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are highly susceptible to backdoor attacks (BAs), wherein training samples are poisoned using trigger-based harmful content. Furthermore, existing defenses have proven ineffective when extensively tested across BA patterns. To better combat BAs, we explore the use of LLM rewriting as a proactive defense against data poisoning. First, we theoretically show that when LLM rewriting utilizes open-book benign samples--termed open-book benign rewriting (OBBR)--the probability of a rewritten output being benign is strictly greater than that of closed-book rewriting. Thus, OBBR neutralizes harmful content by projecting training samples to the space of benign prompts. We then show that, in contrast to previous defenses, OBBR effectively mitigates a large number of existing BAs: across five known BAs and four widely used LLMs, OBBR increases safety performance by an average 51% compared to state-of-the-art BA defenses and 25.7% compared to closed-book rewriting methods. Finally, we show that OBBR is computationally efficient relative to other BA defenses, does not degrade model performance on natural language tasks after fine-tuning, and is capable of defending against non-trigger based data poisoning attacks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: defends against data poisoning via rewriting with benign samples
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过重写方式防御数据投毒，利用良性样本
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过重写方式防御数据投毒，利用良性样本
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过重写方式防御数据投毒，利用良性样本
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes open-book benign rewriting (OBBR) to neutralize harmful content in training data, theoretically guaranteed to increase benign probability. Effective against backdoor attacks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出开放书良性重写（OBBR）方法，利用良性样本重写训练数据以中和有害内容，并理论证明重写后输出为良性的概率增加。实验表明该方法能有效防御多种后门攻击，与安全数据整合防御有害微调的需求高度相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出开放书良性重写（OBBR）方法，利用良性样本重写训练数据以中和有害内容，并理论证明重写后输出为良性的概率增加。实验表明该方法能有效防御多种后门攻击，与安全数据整合防御有害微调的需求高度相关。

quick_skim.title: PoisonForge: Task-Level Targeted Poisoning Benchmark for Instruction-Tuned LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: When practitioners fine-tune LLMs on unvetted datasets, an adversary can exploit the data supply chain through task-level poisoning: inserting a small number of crafted instruction-response pairs that cause the model to embed attacker-specified entities, such as a country, in outputs for a targeted task family while behaving normally elsewhere. We introduce PoisonForge, a benchmark that parameterizes this threat along four dimensions (bias type, poisoning mode, appearance count, and target output length) and evaluates 12 open-weight models (from 2B to 32B parameters) across five families under a primarily 1% poison budget. With only 10 poisoned examples among 1,000 fine-tuning examples, 11 of 12 models exceed a 70% attack success rate (ASR) in their most vulnerable configuration. Meanwhile, unintended leakage to non-target tasks remains below 0.5%, and models perform well on standard benchmarks. We analyze in detail the factors contributing to attack success. We observe that multiple appearances of an entity increase the ASR, the optimal poisoning mode depends on the semantic structure of the target entity, and ASR drops monotonically with the task output length. A correlation analysis and risk prediction model confirm that poisoning design choices, rather than model scale, are the primary causes of attack success, and that these patterns generalize to predict attack success on new tasks. We release all configurations, pipelines, and analysis code to support reproducible comparisons.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for task-level poisoning attacks in LLM fine-tuning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 大语言模型微调任务级投毒攻击基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 大语言模型微调任务级投毒攻击基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 大语言模型微调任务级投毒攻击基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces PoisonForge, a benchmark that parameterizes task-level poisoning attacks during instruction tuning, evaluating 12 models with low poison budgets and high attack success, highlighting the threat of harmful fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出PoisonForge基准，系统性地参数化指令微调中的任务级投毒攻击。通过四种维度刻画威胁，在1%的投毒预算下对12个模型进行评估，发现大多数模型攻击成功率超过70%，揭示了微调数据投毒（有害微调攻击）的严重风险，为防御研究提供了标准化测试平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出PoisonForge基准，系统性地参数化指令微调中的任务级投毒攻击。通过四种维度刻画威胁，在1%的投毒预算下对12个模型进行评估，发现大多数模型攻击成功率超过70%，揭示了微调数据投毒（有害微调攻击）的严重风险，为防御研究提供了标准化测试平台。

quick_skim.title: From Instance Selection to Fixed-Pool Data Recipe Search for Supervised Fine-Tuning
quick_skim.abstract: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) data selection is commonly formulated as instance ranking: score each example and retain a top-$k$ subset. However, effective SFT training subsets are often produced through ordered curation recipes, where filtering, mixing, and deduplication operators jointly shape the final data distribution. We formulate this problem as fixed-pool data recipe search: given a raw instruction pool and a library of grounded operators, the goal is to discover an executable recipe that constructs a high-quality selected subset under a limited budget of full SFT evaluations, without generating, rewriting, or augmenting training samples. We introduce AutoSelection, a two-layer solver that decouples fixed-pool materialization based on cached task-, data-, and model-side signals from expensive full evaluation, using warmup probes, realized subset states, local recipe edits, Gaussian-process-assisted ranking, and stagnation-triggered reseeding. Experiments on a 90K instruction pool show that AutoSelection achieves the strongest in-distribution reasoning average across three base models, outperforming full-data training, random recipe search, random top-$k$, and single-operator selectors. Additional Out-of-distribution graph-reasoning results, search-stability analyses, structural ablations, and 1.5B-to-7B transfer checks further show that recipe structure matters beyond individual selection operators. Code is available at https://github.com/w253/AutoSelection.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: data recipe search with mixing operators for SFT
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 包含混合操作的数据配方搜索用于微调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 包含混合操作的数据配方搜索用于微调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 包含混合操作的数据配方搜索用于微调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AutoSelection, a two-layer solver that searches executable data recipes (including mixing operators) for SFT from a fixed pool, enabling automated curation of high-quality subsets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对监督微调中数据选择问题，提出AutoSelection方法，将数据选择建模为固定池数据配方搜索，通过过滤、混合、去重等操作组合生成高质量子集。在有限评估预算下自动发现最优配方，实验表明其优于传统实例排序方法。该框架可泛化应用于安全数据混合等场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对监督微调中数据选择问题，提出AutoSelection方法，将数据选择建模为固定池数据配方搜索，通过过滤、混合、去重等操作组合生成高质量子集。在有限评估预算下自动发现最优配方，实验表明其优于传统实例排序方法。该框架可泛化应用于安全数据混合等场景。

quick_skim.title: Jailbreak to Protect: Buffering and Reinforcing via Temporary Jailbreaking for Safe Fine-Tuning in Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-tuning-as-a-Service (FaaS) enables personalization of large language models (LLMs), but it can weaken safety-alignment under harmful fine-tuning attacks. Recent work has shown that activating harmful-behavior modules during fine-tuning can prevent models from learning undesired behaviors, but its mechanism remains unclear. In this paper, we revisit temporary jailbreaking as a defense against harmful fine-tuning and provide a gradient-level analysis showing that it saturates safety-degrading gradients while preserving benign task-relevant gradients. Based on this insight, we propose a Buffer-and-Reinforce fine-tuning framework that buffers harmful updates during user fine-tuning and reinforces safety after adaptation. Specifically, BufferLoRA induces temporary jailbreaking as a removable adapter to reduce harmful updates during user fine-tuning. After adaptation, ReinforceLoRA, trained to recover refusal behavior under the temporarily jailbroken state, is integrated with UserLoRA via QR decomposition-based merging to reinforce safety while preserving user-task performance. Extensive experiments show that our framework achieves superior safety and utility with no additional safety data during user fine-tuning and minimal computational cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: defends against harmful fine-tuning by temporary jailbreaking and buffering harmful updates
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过临时越狱和缓冲有害更新来防御有害微调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过临时越狱和缓冲有害更新来防御有害微调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过临时越狱和缓冲有害更新来防御有害微调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Buffer-and-Reinforce framework: temporarily jailbreak to saturate harmful gradients, then reinforce safety after fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对有害微调攻击导致安全对齐削弱问题，提出缓冲增强框架。通过临时越狱激活有害行为模块使有害梯度饱和，从而保留良性任务梯度；微调后强化安全对齐。梯度分析验证该方法能选择性阻断有害更新，同时保持任务性能，为防御有害微调提供新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对有害微调攻击导致安全对齐削弱问题，提出缓冲增强框架。通过临时越狱激活有害行为模块使有害梯度饱和，从而保留良性任务梯度；微调后强化安全对齐。梯度分析验证该方法能选择性阻断有害更新，同时保持任务性能，为防御有害微调提供新思路。

quick_skim.title: On-Policy Consistency Training Improves LLM Safety with Minimal Capability Degradation
quick_skim.abstract: Aligned models can misbehave in several ways: they are often sycophantic, fall victim to jailbreaks, or fail to include appropriate safety warnings. Consistency training is a promising new alignment paradigm to mitigate such failures by training invariants into the model using contrastive input pairs. Existing consistency training procedures generate the supervision signal once, offline, and use supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to update the model. Unfortunately, the resulting models tend to merely memorize the surface forms of the training distribution and thus generalize poorly and regress in their capabilities. We introduce On-Policy Consistency Training (OPCT), a new consistency training approach where the objective is computed over the model's own responses to prompts, supervised by itself conditioned on corresponding contrastive prompts. We evaluate OPCT on three safety axes: sycophancy, jailbreaking, and safety awareness. Across three model families, OPCT outperforms its SFT counterpart on all safety desiderata. It nearly halves the sycophancy rate relative to baseline (8.1% vs. 15.4%, compared to 11.2% for SFT). Under an adaptive per-target attacker, OPCT holds jailbreak defense success near 99% on held-out jailbreak behaviors, whereas SFT achieves 87% on average. On safety awareness, OPCT outperforms SFT in two out of three models, and matches it on the other. OPCT also largely avoids the capability regressions that SFT induces, such as a 28-point drop on MATH-500. Our results suggest that consistency training is best implemented as OPCT rather than as SFT, especially when generalization beyond the training distribution is desired.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: improves LLM safety via on-policy consistency training to mitigate jailbreaks and sycophancy
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过在线策略一致性训练提升LLM安全，减轻越狱和谄媚
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过在线策略一致性训练提升LLM安全，减轻越狱和谄媚
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过在线策略一致性训练提升LLM安全，减轻越狱和谄媚
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: OPCT computes consistency objective over current model outputs, avoiding offline supervision memorization, and improves safety with minimal capability loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有离线一致性训练导致泛化差、能力退化问题，提出在线策略一致性训练(OPCT)。在模型当前输出上计算一致性目标，而非固定离线数据。实验表明OPCT显著提升模型对越狱和谄媚的防御能力，同时保持下游任务性能几乎不受影响，为安全微调提供了更高效的对齐范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有离线一致性训练导致泛化差、能力退化问题，提出在线策略一致性训练(OPCT)。在模型当前输出上计算一致性目标，而非固定离线数据。实验表明OPCT显著提升模型对越狱和谄媚的防御能力，同时保持下游任务性能几乎不受影响，为安全微调提供了更高效的对齐范式。
